problem: How to produce defect-free large-area graphene at low cost?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) may resolve How to produce defect-free large-area graphene at low cost? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Prototype device testing with 26 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
